MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Kime H. Smith of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (925-947-1300), appearing for defendant Oltmans Construction Co.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Defendant Oltmans Construction Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Plaintiffs did not file any exhibits other than Exhibit A attached to the declaration of Philip M. Aelstyn. Accordingly, the Court did not consider the exhibits referenced by Plaintiffs in their response to Defendant's separate statement.

Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Between 1969 and 1972, Decedent recalled three jobsites where he saw Oltmans employees working. (Defendant's Index, Ex. P at 636:7-12). He recalled seeing signs outside the big trailers that said "Oltmans Construction" and also recalled seeing the Oltmans name on hard hats. (Defendant's Index, EX. O at 229:7-11; 14-22.) He recalled seeing the names Georgia Pacific, USG, and Hamilton on the boxes that Oltmans' crew were working with. (Id. at 638:7-19.) He identified the brands by their logo. (Id. at 638:20-25.) He testified that Hamilton had a red dot, and he knew they were working with it because they would take it out of the bags and put it in their bazookas-that was their topping and they had two: one was in a green box, and one was in a red box. (Id. at 638:24-639:5.) Plaintiff "has no obligation to prove a specific exposure to a specific product on a specific date or time." (Turley v. Familian Corp. (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 969, 985.)

That decedent had no knowledge of asbestos content is insufficient to demonstrate that Plaintiffs lack the ability to produce such evidence. Defendant fails to address the PMK witnesses identified by Plaintiffs including Charles W. Lehnert, Howard Bowman, or Willis Hamilton. (Defendant's Index, Ex. H at 15:9-14; 16:4-13; 16:19-23.) Defendant also failed to show reasonable diligence in contacting co-worker witnesses Robert Anderson and Carol Brown, identified by Plaintiffs in their response to Defendant's special interrogatories. (Defendant's Index, Ex. H at 3:8-11; 5:20-25.) The declaration of Bruce Robertson states that he found 68 results for Robert Anderson, which Defendant failed to follow up upon. (Defendant's Index, Ex. T at par. 4.) Further, Defendant did not address whether it only attempted to locate "Caroll Brown," as identified in Plaintiffs' Product Identification Witnesses and List of Former Testimony, and not "Carol Brown." (Defendant's Index, Ex. M at 3:22-24.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION To Plaintiffs Complaint For Survival, Wrongful Death?Asbestos
Off Calendar Nov-23-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Dominique Marangoni-Simonsen of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant A.H. Voss Company.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.

RULING:
Matter not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant A.H. Voss Company's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant filed and served a Notice of Errata regarding an Amended Notice of Motion on November 10, 2022, after Plaintiff filed and served the opposition on November 9, 2022. In its Notice of Errata, Defendant asked the Court to consider the Amended Notice of Motion. (Passalacqua Supplemental Decl., Ex. A at 2:1.) The Court did not consider the Amended Notice of Motion as it was untimely filed. Defendant raised two new issues on reply regarding the primary rights doctrine and special verdicts as legal findings. The golden rule of summary adjudication is that if it is not set forth in the separate statement, then it does not exist. (See United Community Church v. Garcin (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 327, 337 (superseded by statute on other grounds).) These issues are not in Defendant's separate statement. The Court did not address these issues.

Defendant failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b), which requires that the specific cause of action be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated verbatim in the separate statement of undisputed material facts, and CRC 3.1350(d)(1)(A), which requires that the separate statement of undisputed material facts in support of the motion separately identify each cause of action that is the subject of the motion. Defendant further failed to sustain its initial burden of showing that the injury in this wrongful death suit is the same as the injury in the personal injury suit for collateral estoppel to apply as a complete defense. (See Grinham v. Fielder (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 1049, 1053-1054.) The injury alleged in the personal injury suit was asbestosis and asbestos related pleural disease. (Passalacqua Decl., Ex. A at 8:8-12; 57:6-9.) The verdict against Defendant in the personal injury suit was decided by a jury in November 2016. (Passalacqua Decl., Ex. C at 2-6.) The injury alleged in the wrongful death suit is death caused by lung cancer. (Passalacqua Decl., Ex. B at 7:24-8:6.) Decedent was diagnosed with lung cancer in March 2020, and died from lung cancer in May 2020, almost four years after the personal injury verdict. (Passalacqua Decl., Ex. B at 7:24-26, 8:3-6.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
